description: Evaluation queries for the Homework #2 retrieval layer. `category` records why each query is in the set; `expected_documents` is the document_id(s) a good retriever should surface, used for a mechanical hit/miss verdict. `comment` is authored by hand AFTER inspecting a real run — it is the graded relevance judgement and must never be a placeholder.

queries.query: What is a backhaul and why does it matter to a carrier?
How does CQRS separate the write model from the read models?
What is the strangler-fig pattern for migrating a monolith?
How do I stop my trucks from driving back empty after a delivery?
Why would you keep every change as a permanent record instead of just updating a row in a table?
How can I release new code without users noticing any interruption?
How is data kept consistent between the old system and the new services during a migration?
What has to be true for the platform to handle 5,000 requests per second?
Who confirms that a delivery actually happened, and what does that confirmation trigger?
What is the best way to fine-tune a large language model on a custom dataset?
